name: Lakshmi Devi
title: Master Kundan Artisan
bio: Lakshmi learned kundan setting from her mother at age twelve, in a workshop tucked behind the old city walls. Thirty years later, her hands still set each stone with the same unhurried precision — she says the lac must breathe before the foil is laid.
quote: A stone knows when it has found its place. My job is to listen.

name: Farida Bano
title: Zari & Textile Embroiderer
bio: Farida specialises in chikankari-inspired zari work for our clothing jewellery line. She works from a sunlit room where silk is stretched on frames and the only sound is her needle passing through cloth — a rhythm she has kept for twenty-five years.
quote: Thread carries intention. I embroider as if the wearer is in the room.

name: Rajesh Kumar
title: Silversmith & Bangle Maker
bio: Rajesh trained as a silversmith in the traditional workshops of north Kolkata. He hammers each bangle by hand, believing that machine uniformity strips jewellery of soul. His hammer marks are signatures, not flaws.
quote: Metal remembers every strike. I strike with purpose.

name: Meenakshi Iyer
title: Pearl Stringer & Necklace Designer
bio: Meenakshi comes from a family of pearl merchants and stringers. She matches pearls by lustre in natural morning light — never under lamps — and knots silk thread between each gem so a broken strand never loses more than one pearl.
quote: A necklace should fall like water. Pearls must agree with each other first.

name: Hassan Qureshi
title: Enamel Artist
bio: Hassan paints enamel with brushes made from squirrel hair, mixing pigments the way his grandfather taught him. His gulmohar blooms on our kada bands are fired twice — the second firing deepens the red until it resembles flame-tree petals at dusk.
quote: Colour is not decoration. It is the emotion of the piece.
